title: ANM Apo/Holo Flexibility Comparison — AOH1996 Cryptic Pocket
method: Anisotropic Network Model (ANM), Atilgan et al. 2001
interpretation: Apo PCNA (1W60): pocket residues are 14% LESS flexible than background (fold-change 0.857), consistent with a rigidly packed region. Holo PCNA (8GLA): pocket residues are 10% MORE flexible than background (fold-change 1.104). Delta fold-change = +0.247, consistent with a ligand-associated flexibility increase at this site. ANM is a physics-based approximation on static coordinates; it does not prove apo-to-holo pocket opening or confirm cryptic-pocket status. MD simulation is required for that conclusion.
scientific_note: The apo->holo flexibility difference (fold-change delta +0.247) is consistent with structural signatures sometimes seen at cryptic sites, but does not confirm the AOH1996 site is a genuine cryptic pocket. ANM does not simulate ligand-induced dynamics. This result is hypothesis-supporting, not confirmatory.
